error.title: Couldn't reach durin
error.gatewayHint: Make sure the gateway is running (`durin gateway`) and that this page is open on the same machine.

auth.title: Authentication required
auth.hint: Enter the secret configured as tokenIssueSecret in your gateway config.

account.logoutHint: Disconnect this browser from the gateway.

system.restartHint: Restart durin to apply runtime changes.

meta.description: durin web UI — chat with your durin workspace.

help.theme: Switch between light and dark appearance.
help.palette: durin's colour identity — Ithildin, Forge, or Mithril.
help.language: Choose the language used by the WebUI.
help.provider: Select the provider that should serve new model requests.
help.model: Set the default model name used by durin.
help.configPath: The gateway configuration file currently in use.

byok.description: Bring your own provider keys. durin reads these values from the current config and only configured providers can be selected in General.
byok.webSearchDescription: Configure the web search provider and its credential. This only affects the web search tool.
byok.apiKeyConfiguredPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep the current key
byok.apiKeyRequired: API key is required to configure this provider.
byok.configureFirst: Configure a provider in BYOK first.

webSearch.providerHelp: Choose the backend used by the web search tool.
webSearch.noCredentialHelp: DuckDuckGo works without a saved API key.
webSearch.apiKeyHelp: Stored in config and masked after saving.
webSearch.baseUrlHelp: SearXNG needs the URL of your own instance.
webSearch.apiKeyRequired: API key is required for this search provider.
webSearch.baseUrlRequired: Base URL is required for SearXNG.
webSearch.missingCredential: Add the required credential before saving.
webSearch.saveHint: Changes apply to new web search requests.

secrets.title: Secrets
secrets.description: API keys and tokens, stored in ~/.durin/secrets.json (mode 0600), outside config.json. Values are never shown here.
secrets.hintName: UPPER_SNAKE — also the environment variable name in exec.
secrets.hintService: What the secret is for, e.g. atlassian or provider:openai.
secrets.hintScope: Comma-separated consumer tags: exec, skill:*, channel:telegram.
secrets.hintValue: Leave blank when editing to keep the stored value.
secrets.hintValueEdit: Leave blank to keep the current value. Provide a new value only when rotating the secret.

config.description: Every durin setting, grouped by section. Editing a value writes it straight to the config — the same as durin config set. Lists and managed secrets are read-only here.
config.secretRotateHint: Write a new value to the stored secret. All config refs pointing to this name will use it.
config.secretDisconnectHint: Replace this ref with a plaintext input. The secret stays in the store, only this field is severed.
config.secretRotateDescription: Replace the value stored for {{name}}. Every config field that references ${secret:{{name}}} will pick this up immediately. The value never leaves the secret store and never enters the chat.

models.auxDescription: durin uses these only when the main model can't handle images or audio itself.
models.subagentModelHint: Model used by spawned subagents (background tasks). Same as default when unset.
models.memoryHint: Used by the memory dream — the background pass that consolidates and curates stored memories. Falls back to the main model when unset.
models.judgeHint: Model and provider used by the skill security LLM judge (and the skill composition gate). They travel as a pair; when unset, the default model runs.
models.testHint: Run a real round-trip against the configured model.
models.loadError: Could not load the model configuration.
models.capsUnknown: Pick a model to see what it supports.
models.testRowHint: Real round-trip to the configured model — verifies the API key and model id work together.
models.contextWindowHint: Max input tokens fed to the model per turn. Defaults to the model catalog value.
models.temperatureHint: Sampling temperature in [0, 2]. Lower = more deterministic.
models.maxTokensHint: Cap per assistant message. Bounded by the model.
models.reasoningEffortHint: For reasoning models: low | medium | high. Leave blank for default.
models.defaultModelHint: Provider and model used for all new requests.

channels.description: Bridge durin to chat platforms. Enable a channel and set its credential — the token goes to the secret store, not config.json.
channels.consentLabel: I consent to durin reading this mailbox
channels.needsInstall: Optional dependency required — installs automatically when enabled (durin-ai[{{extra}}])
channels.installOnRestart: Will install on next gateway restart (durin-ai[{{extra}}])

telegram.newBotHint: Send /newbot and copy the token it gives you.
telegram.pairingInstructionGeneric: Open your bot in Telegram and send /start to pair your account.
telegram.noPairings: No pending or approved pairings yet.
telegram.allowFromHint: Comma-separated IDs or @usernames. * = everyone (public bot). Empty = pairing only.

slack.step1Hint: Choose "From a manifest", pick your workspace and paste the manifest — it provisions all scopes, events and Socket Mode in one step.
slack.step2Hint: In Basic Information → App-Level Tokens, generate a token with the connections:write scope and paste it here.
slack.step3Hint: In Install App, install the app to your workspace and paste the Bot User OAuth Token.
slack.pairingInstruction: Send the bot a direct message in Slack — unapproved senders automatically receive a pairing code you can approve here.
slack.noPairings: No pending or approved pairings yet.
slack.enabledWithoutTokens: The channel is enabled but has no working tokens — complete the steps below to finish the setup.

errors.swapped_tokens: the tokens look swapped — xoxb- goes in the bot field, xapp- in the app field
errors.not_allowed_token_type: wrong token type for this field
errors.invalid_auth: Slack rejected the token (invalid or revoked)
errors.missing_scope: the token is missing a required scope — recreate the app from the manifest
errors.not_configured: the channel has no saved tokens yet

slack.channelsHint: Join the bot to the channels where you want to mention it. Private channels need a manual /invite @bot from inside Slack.
slack.noChannels: No channels visible to the bot yet.
slack.step4Hint: Stores both tokens as durin secrets and starts the channel.
slack.statusChecking: Checking the stored tokens against Slack…
slack.secretsStored: Tokens are stored as the SLACK_BOT_TOKEN and SLACK_APP_TOKEN secrets — the config only holds references.
slack.runtimeChecking: Checking whether the channel is running…
slack.runtimeRunning: Channel running — connected to Slack over Socket Mode.
slack.runtimeStopped: Channel stopped — messages will not arrive until it starts.

discord.step1Hint: Create a new application, add a bot to it, and come back with its token.
discord.step2Hint: In the Bot page, reveal (or reset) the token and paste it here.
discord.permissionLimitedHint: Works now, but Discord revokes this once the bot joins more than 100 servers unless the app gets verified.
discord.permissionDisabledHint: Message Content Intent is off in the Bot page — turn it on, then re-check.
discord.step3Title: Invite the bot to a server
discord.step3Hint: Copy the invite link or open it directly to add the bot to one of your servers.
discord.invitePermissions: Grants only what durin needs: view channels, send messages, embed links, attach files, read message history, and add reactions — never Administrator.
discord.enabledWithoutTokens: The channel is enabled but has no working token — complete the steps below to finish the setup.
discord.statusChecking: Checking the stored token against Discord…
discord.secretsStored: The token is stored as the DISCORD_TOKEN secret — the config only holds a reference.
discord.runtimeChecking: Checking whether the channel is running…
discord.runtimeStopped: Channel stopped — messages will not arrive until it starts.

errors.not_configured: the channel has no saved token yet
errors.invalid_token: Discord rejected the token (invalid or revoked)
errors.unauthorized: Discord rejected the token (invalid or revoked)
errors.rate_limited: Discord is rate-limiting these requests — try again shortly
errors.forbidden: the bot lacks permission for this request

discord.pairingInstruction: Send the bot a direct message in Discord — unapproved senders automatically receive a pairing code you can approve here.
discord.noPairings: No pending or approved pairings yet.
discord.scopeAll: Answer in every channel it can see
discord.scopeOnly: Only in the channels I choose
discord.scopeOnlyWarning: With no channels ticked, durin answers everywhere it can see. Tick the channels you want to restrict it to.
discord.noGuilds: The bot is not in any server yet — invite it first.

whatsapp.banRiskWarning: WhatsApp pairing uses an unofficial protocol, not WhatsApp's Business API. Meta can ban the number for automated use — pair a dedicated number, never your primary one.
whatsapp.scanInstruction: Open WhatsApp on your phone → Settings → Linked Devices → Link a device, then scan this code. It refreshes automatically until scanned.
whatsapp.timeout: The QR code expired without being scanned.
whatsapp.runtimeChecking: Checking whether the channel is running…
whatsapp.runtimeStopped: Channel stopped — messages will not arrive until it starts.

desc.websocket: Transport for the web dashboard and external WebSocket clients. Always on while the dashboard is enabled. The token is optional, only for external clients.
desc.email: Receive and reply to email over IMAP (inbound) and SMTP (outbound).
desc.slack: Chat with durin in Slack DMs, channels and threads over Socket Mode — no public URL needed.
desc.discord: Chat with durin in Discord servers and direct messages.
desc.whatsapp: Chat with durin over WhatsApp by pairing a phone number with a QR code.

fieldHelp.threading_mode: Per mail thread: each email thread becomes its own conversation — best when the same person writes about different topics. Per sender: all mail from an address continues one long conversation.

memory.description: Tune the memory subsystem — search reranking and dream auto-consolidation.

help.crossEncoderEnabled: Opt-in second-pass reranker. Adds 300-1500 ms per query and ~1 GB RAM and can improve quality on ambiguous queries. Off by default.
help.crossEncoderModel: Model used when the reranker is enabled. The default works well for multilingual queries; alternatives trade size for latency.
help.dreamEnabled: Master switch for the automatic dream. When off, the cron and reactive triggers are disabled; the manual `durin memory dream` still works.
help.dreamCron: Cron expression for the daily extract / refine / skill pass. Default 0 3 * * * (3am local).
help.dreamPostCompaction: Run a dream pass after a session is compacted — the context is already in memory so the cost is amortised.
help.dreamOnSessionClose: Run a dream pass when a session ends (/quit or idle timeout).
help.dreamDiscover: Also create entities from durable facts you mention but don't explicitly save, so memory grows from conversation — not only from explicit saves. On by default.
help.dreamAutoAbsorb: Let the refine pass automatically merge entities it judges duplicates (high-confidence only). Off keeps merges manual.
help.dreamAbsorbConfidence: Minimum confidence (0-100) the judge requires to merge two entities. 95 = high precision. Only applies when auto-absorb is on.
help.dreamAbsorbSemanticThreshold: How close (embedding distance, ~0–1; 0.20 ≈ 90% similar) two same-type entities must be for the judge to consider them the same. Lower = stricter. The judge still decides the actual merge.
help.dreamThrottle: Minimum seconds between reactive dream runs (post-compaction / session-close). Absorbs bursts so overlapping passes don't pile up; 0 disables. The daily cron is never throttled.
help.dreamMaxSeconds: Hard wall-clock cap per extract pass. When reached, the pass yields after the current session and the per-session cursor resumes the remainder on the next trigger. 0 = unbounded.
help.dreamAlwaysOnBudget: Token budget for the 'always-on' guidance pinned into every prompt. The dream ranks feedback (stance/practice), drops contradictions, and fits the highest-priority items into this budget. 0 disables the pin.

memory.dreamDescription: The dream consolidates sessions into entity pages + skills and dedups duplicate entities. It runs on a daily cron plus optional reactive triggers.

skillsSecurity.description: Security policy for importing skills: the LLM judge, trust patterns, the GitHub token, and size caps.

help.judgeTrigger: Off: only on-demand (the 'Audit with LLM' button per skill). Uncertain: auto-run only when the gate is already unsure (code / caution / out-of-allowlist). Always: every import. Degrades silently when no model is available.
help.judgeMaxSeverity: How high the judge may raise a verdict. 'Caution' forces a confirm but never blocks on its own; 'Dangerous' lets it block (override required).
help.judgeModel: Model name for the judge. Empty uses the default (main) model.
help.tokenSecret: The durin secret holding your GitHub token. 'None' = anonymous requests (lower rate limit, no private repos).

skillsSecurity.trustDescription: A safe skill from a trusted source installs without a confirm. Patterns match the source by prefix — e.g. github:acme/, https://gitlab.com/acme/.
skillsSecurity.tokenDescription: A GitHub token raises the API rate limit and reaches private repos. Pick a secret holding the token (create one in the Secrets section).

cron.title: Scheduled jobs
cron.description: Background tasks the cron service runs on a schedule. Add a job from this panel or via the `cron` tool in chat. System jobs (dream, memory dream) cannot be removed but can be disabled.
cron.emptyHint: The agent can create one via the `cron` tool from chat.
cron.modeHelp: Reminder: the message is delivered as a brief natural message. Task: the agent executes the message with full tools and delivers the result only when useful.
cron.fieldToHelp: The destination ID for the chosen channel (e.g. a chat ID, phone number, or @user — depends on the channel).

memory_dream.name: Entity memory consolidation
memory_dream.note: Daily pass (3am local) plus reactive triggers (post-compaction / session-close) that processes recent sessions — extracts entity attributes, mines skills, deduplicates entities, and distills the always-on pin.

modes.title: Modes
modes.subtitle: Which tools the agent can use, and with what posture
modes.descriptionPlaceholder: Reads and comments, applies no changes
modes.promptPlaceholder: Empty is fine. Describe the posture only when the tool filter cannot.
modes.fullAccessNote: Full access — every tool, current and future.
modes.onlyHelp: The agent can use ONLY the checked tools.
modes.exceptHelp: The agent can use every tool EXCEPT the checked ones.
modes.addByName: add a tool by name, Enter to add
modes.catalogEmpty: Tool catalog unavailable — type tool names manually.

personas.soulsDescription: Reusable SOUL documents — the system identity a persona adopts. The `default` SOUL is built in and cannot be removed.
personas.soulsEmptyHint: Add one with the New soul form above.
personas.personasDescription: Named presets that pair a SOUL with an optional model and description. The default persona is used for new sessions.
personas.personasEmptyHint: Add one with the New persona form above.

providerModels.title: models
providerModels.emptyTeach: No catalog models for this provider yet. Add a custom one below.

providers.localHint: Local server — no API key needed

github.title: GitHub
github.usedBy: Used by skills, MCP discovery, and the provider
github.forgetConfirm: Forget GitHub? Skills and MCP lose access.

oauth.browserOpened: A browser window was opened to approve with {{provider}}.
oauth.timeout: Authorization timed out. Try connecting again.

openrouter.description: Get an API key by approving with your OpenRouter account — no copy-paste.

codex.deviceCodeHint: If you see a “device code authorization” error, enable it in your ChatGPT security settings and try again.

mcp.description: Connect Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers to give the agent extra tools. Status is read live from the running gateway.
mcp.hintName: A unique id for this server (used as the tool prefix).
mcp.hintType: Leave on auto to infer from command/url.
mcp.hintCommand: Stdio: the executable to run (e.g. npx).
mcp.hintToolTimeout: Seconds before a tool call is cancelled.
mcp.hintEnabledTools: Comma-separated tool names, or * for all. Reconnect to apply.
mcp.hintOauth: Mark this server as requiring OAuth sign-in.
mcp.hintAllowPrivateUrl: Opt out of the SSRF private-IP block for this server.
mcp.hintSpawnEgress: Action when a stdio server looks like a shell + network-egress shape.
mcp.hintMalwareCheck: Query the OSV API for advisories before spawning (stdio).
mcp.errorConflict: That server already exists, or a sign-in is already in progress.
mcp.reconnectHint: Reconnect to retry this server or apply config changes.
mcp.emptyHint: Add one to give the agent extra tools.
mcp.autoHint: Inferred from the command or URL. Fill in whichever applies.
mcp.hintOauthClientId: Optional. For servers without dynamic registration (e.g. GitHub): from a registered OAuth app.
mcp.hintOauthClientSecret: Optional. Paired with the Client ID for static OAuth registration.

discover.searchPlaceholder: Search MCP servers (e.g. jira, postgres, github)…
discover.fillRequired: Fill in all required fields to continue.
discover.runtimeMissingDocker: This server runs in a Docker container, but Docker isn't installed on this machine.
discover.runtimeMissing: This server needs {{runtime}}, which isn't installed on this machine.

voice.intro: When you attach or record audio, durin transcribes it to text before it reaches the agent. The default is local STT via sherpa-onnx (offline). Switch to a cloud provider or any OpenAI-compatible HTTP server as needed.
voice.providerDesc: Local STT needs the [stt] extra; cloud providers need an API key.

engineDesc.parakeet: Parakeet TDT v3 — 25 European languages incl. Spanish / English, fastest. For Japanese / Chinese, use SenseVoice or a cloud provider.
engineDesc.sensevoice: SenseVoice — optimized for Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Cantonese, and English.

localStt.title: Local STT (sherpa-onnx)
localStt.installedDesc: ✓ sherpa-onnx is installed. Fast local transcription ready.
localStt.missingDesc: Not installed — local transcription won't work until you add [stt].

http.keyDesc: Only if the server requires auth.

voice.enabledDesc: Master switch — when off, audio is never transcribed.
voice.modeDesc: Auto inserts text you can edit; Preview requires explicit accept; Off attaches raw audio.
voice.languageHintDesc: ISO-639-1 code (es, en, ja, zh…). Empty = auto-detect.

tts.providerDesc: Voice output engine for conversational mode.
tts.localInstallDesc: Adds the [tts] extra for on-device speech synthesis.
tts.openaiKeyDesc: Empty = uses the OPENAI_API_KEY env var.
tts.localInstalledDesc: ✓ Supertonic is installed. On-device speech synthesis ready.

conv.handsFreeDesc: The floating orb listens, thinks and speaks.
conv.bargeInDesc: Interrupt the agent by speaking over it.
conv.idleTimeoutDesc: End the voice session after this much silence. The timer resets when you speak or the assistant replies.

spoken.longRepliesDesc: What the voice speaks when a reply is long. "Opening + rest on screen" speaks the first part and keeps the full text on screen; "Read full reply" speaks everything.

preview.preparing: Still preparing the voice (the model may be downloading) — try again in a moment.

concurrency.description: How many turns and sub-agents run at once. Changes apply immediately, no restart.

help.interactive: Max interactive turns running at once across all sessions.
help.ceiling: Max concurrent work overall — interactive turns plus sub-agents.

workflows.title: Workflows
workflows.empty: No workflows yet — click + to create one.
workflows.nameExists: A workflow with that name already exists.
workflows.duplicateHint: Copy this workflow to a new name, to use as a starting point
workflows.improvementHint_manual: Manual: the dream proposes improvements (a node prompt or a gate's criterion) for you to review and apply.
workflows.improvementHint_auto: Auto: the dream applies prompt-scoped improvements itself (validated, versioned, and auto-reverted if the next runs get worse). Structural ideas still come to you.
workflows.selectPrompt: Select a workflow to view its graph.
workflows.taskPlaceholder: Task to run through this workflow…
workflows.recCopied: Context copied — paste it in a chat to work the idea with the agent.
workflows.recCopyFailed: Could not copy to the clipboard.
workflows.recStructuralHint: Out of the dream's scope (prompts, plus failing-script command/file edits) — never auto-applied; treat it in a session
workflows.context_own: own (isolated — sees only the previous step)
workflows.context_shared: shared (reads and extends the shared context)
workflows.commandPlaceholder: e.g. pytest -q — stdin receives the previous step's output
workflows.scriptNameHint: Single filename, no folders (e.g. check.py or gate.sh)
workflows.scriptLoadError: Could not load the script's content.
workflows.parallelNeedBranch: Pick at least one branch to run in parallel.
workflows.parallelNeedWorkerList: Pick a worker template and the node whose output is the list.
workflows.ioInputHint: What this workflow accepts as input.
workflows.ioDescriptionPlaceholder: e.g. a CSV with columns date, amount
workflows.outputFormatLabel: deliver as (optional, overrides the output for this run)
workflows.outputFormatPlaceholder: e.g. a bulleted list, JSON with fields x,y, a 3-line summary
workflows.historyRetention: history and working folders keep the most recent runs (workflow.keep_runs)
workflows.needsInputBody: The run paused at {{node}} — it did not fail. Answer below to continue this same run.
workflows.resumeCaption: resumes at {{node}} · same working folder and sessions · run {{runId}}
workflows.outputPruneHint: pruned after older runs accumulate — copy out what you need
workflows.persistentInParallelWarning: This node is a branch of {{parallelId}} — persistent sessions aren't allowed on parallel units. Saving will fail.

definitions.empty: No loops yet — click New loop to create one.

form.name: Name
form.noTriggersHint: Manual-only loop — no scheduled triggers. It can still be fired manually.
form.semanticHint: Judged by the aux model on every matching message — leave empty to skip.
form.correlateHint: Regex with one capture group — messages with the same captured value reach the same run.
form.matchWakeOrNew: Wake the waiting run when the thread matches
form.checksSufficientHint: When every required check passes, the goal counts as reached without asking the model. Script checks only.
form.stuckAfterHint: Consecutive no-progress checks before escalating to you.
form.operatorToHint: For chat channels (Telegram, Slack…) escalations need a recipient id; without it they only reach the Activity inbox.

skills.modeAutoHint: Auto: the dream applies its improvements to this skill automatically.
skills.modeManualHint: Manual: the dream proposes edits for your approval; only you change it.
skills.empty: No skills yet. Add one to get started.
skills.selectPrompt: Select a skill to view it, or add a new one.
skills.acquireHint: Import from a path, URL, or repo, or search the registry. Every skill you bring in waits in Pending until you approve it.
skills.lastUsedApprox: Approximate — based on session activity, not the exact call time.

history.diffEmpty: No file changes in this commit.
history.diffError: Could not load the changes. Collapse and expand again to retry.

skills.unscannedHint: Not analyzed yet. Run “Audit with LLM” to review it.

reason.untrusted_source: The source {{detail}} isn't in your trusted allowlist, so it needs your explicit approval.
reason.carries_code: It carries executable code ({{detail}}) — review before approving.
reason.verdict_caution: The security scan flagged it as caution.
reason.verdict_dangerous: The security scan flagged it as dangerous.

audit.clean: The AI reviewed it and found no problems.
audit.unreachable: Couldn't reach the audit model. Check your connection and retry.
audit.parse: The audit response was unreadable. Retry.

review.confirmSafe: Mark this skill reviewed and safe?
review.confirmDangerous: This skill has a DANGEROUS finding. Mark it reviewed and safe anyway?

import.placeholder: Import a skill: path, https://…/SKILL.md, or github:owner/repo
import.alreadyInstalled: {{name}} is already installed — re-install to override.
import.confirmInstall: This skill carries code or is flagged caution. Install it anyway?
import.forceDangerous: This skill is DANGEROUS — the scan found a serious risk. Force install anyway?
import.replaceExists: A skill with this name is already installed. Replace it?
import.trusted: Trusted {{prefix}} — future safe imports from it skip the confirm.
import.unresolved: Could not resolve a skill from that source. Ask the agent in chat to investigate the link.
import.blockedDesc: Some dependencies can't be auto-installed. You can resolve them later.

preview.unavailable: Preview unavailable — you can still import.

search.acquireExplainer: Public-registry skills. Importing drops a skill into Pending for your approval.

skills.title: Skills

empty.greeting: What can I do for you?

icon.title: Design an app icon
icon.prompt: Generate a clean 1:1 app icon for durin: friendly robot, simple vector style, soft blue and white palette, no text.

sticker.title: Make a sticker
sticker.prompt: Generate a cute sticker-style image of a tiny robot assistant, transparent-looking background, expressive and playful.

poster.title: Create a poster
poster.prompt: Generate a polished poster concept for a personal AI assistant, modern composition, strong visual hierarchy, suitable for a landing page.

product.title: Product mockup
product.prompt: Generate a clean product mockup image for a conversational AI web app, minimal interface, premium lighting, realistic device frame.

portrait.title: Stylized portrait
portrait.prompt: Generate a stylized portrait of a friendly AI companion, soft lighting, detailed but approachable, modern illustration style.

edit.title: Edit an image
edit.prompt: Help me edit an image. Ask me to upload or reference the image first, then generate the edited result.

empty.description: Ask questions, continue your local work, or start a new thread.

new.title: New chat
new.description: Stop the current task and start a fresh conversation.

stop.title: Stop current task
stop.description: Cancel the active agent turn for this chat.

restart.title: Restart durin
restart.description: Restart the bot process in place.

status.title: Show status
status.description: Display runtime, provider, and channel status.

effort.title: Reasoning effort
effort.description: Show or set the reasoning effort for the active model.

usage.title: Token usage
usage.description: Show cumulative token usage for this session.

retry.title: Retry last message
retry.description: Send the previous user message again.

dream.title: Run Dream
dream.description: Manually run the entity consolidation dream (extract / skill / refine / always_on).

dream_log.title: Show Dream log
dream_log.description: Show what the last Dream consolidation changed.

dream_restore.title: Restore memory
dream_restore.description: Revert memory to a previous Dream snapshot.

goal.title: Long-running goal
goal.description: Tell the agent to treat this as a sustained multi-step goal.

imageRejected.magic_mismatch: File doesn't look like a real image
imageRejected.too_large: Image is too large — try a smaller one

documentRejected.too_large: Document is too large — 25 MB max

askUser.placeholder: Type your answer, or pick an option to edit

reqSecret.stored: Saved — the value never reached the model
reqSecret.hint: Stored straight to durin's secret store; the value never reaches the model or the chat.

plan.title: Proposed plan
plan.hint: or reply with feedback to refine it

message.queuedChip: Queued — read when the current work finishes

messageTooBig.title: Message too large
messageTooBig.body: The server rejected your last message because it exceeded the size limit. Remove some images or try smaller files, then send again.

apiStatus.givingUpBody: The error response will appear in the chat below. You can resend the message to retry.
apiStatus.exhaustedBody: Too many identical errors in a row — giving up to avoid an infinite loop.
apiStatus.transientBody: Transient provider error; the request will be re-sent automatically.

logs.title: Logs
logs.empty: No log lines in this window.

memoryGraph.title: Memory
memoryGraph.interactionHint: Click another node, drag to pin, or use the focus icon to isolate 1-hop neighbours.
memoryGraph.noSourceLinkedEntries: No episodic entries linked back to this session via source_refs.
memoryGraph.emptyHint: Run durin memory dream once entries accumulate.
memoryGraph.noConsolidatedHint: Mentioned in episodic entries but no canonical entity page yet. Run durin memory dream to extract and create one.
memoryGraph.documentsEmpty: No documents yet. Attach one in chat or run memory_ingest to add it to your library.
memoryGraph.documentsHint: Pick a document to see its outline, distilled entities, and content.
memoryGraph.documentForgetBody: It's archived and removed from search — the agent won't surface it. You can re-ingest it later.
memoryGraph.documentNotDistilled: Not distilled yet — the nightly dream will summarise this and seed entities.
memoryGraph.documentEntitiesIntro: Entities related to this document, grouped by how they connect to it:
memoryGraph.documentRelDistilledHint: The nightly dream extracted these from the document's content — the document is about them.
memoryGraph.documentRelReferencedHint: These cited the document as a source during a conversation — the document doesn't necessarily describe them (e.g. a patient whose workup used this paper).

entries.empty: No entries reference this entity yet.
entries.cantArchiveProtected: Can't archive entity pages from here — use durin memory absorb / revert.
entries.headline: Headline
entries.author: Author

composer.hint: Enter to send · Shift+Enter for newline

empty.noChatsBody: Start a conversation — your sessions are stored locally on the durin workspace and stay available across reloads.

dream.title: Dream
dream.runningBanner: The dream is running — events appear here live.
dream.runningEmpty: Working… the first results will appear shortly.
dream.runFailed: The dream run finished with errors.

bandeja.emptyFlagged: No memory pairs flagged for review.
